claim: Metal backend byte parity with the CPU reference of this package, complete native 4000 frame (validation frame2), established in-process against the compiled cpu-fast backend and bound to the pinned CPU-reference output of the checked-in receipts
parity_baseline: compared bitwise in-process against ComputeBackend.CPU_FAST (output bytes, all counters, RNG accounting, startup receipt, and the three diagnostics planes); the output hash equals the CPU-reference hash pinned by the cuda and cpu-fast receipts for this fixture, and output plus all three diagnostics planes equal the archived full-frame export of the completed CUDA gate bitwise, so the chain to the CPU reference is hash-transitive on identical input bytes

metal_runtime.writer_chain_location: host CPU, one core, via the compiled fast_cpu.kernels.write_band path (cuda_backend/host_writer.py), the same host writer the CUDA backend uses
metal_runtime.load_note: 1-minute load average 22.5 on this 10-core machine during the gate; byte equality is load-independent, wall time is not

stage_timings_note: see docs/metal-backend.md for the stage table
